chemistry.reaction_name: Visible-Light Driven Photocascade Catalysis: Union of N,N‑Dimethylanilines and α‑Azidochalcones
chemistry.bond_formed: one C-C and two C-N bonds
chemistry.scope_summary: 20 different 1,3-diazabicyclo[3.1.0]hexanes synthesized in 55-71% yields. Works with various N,N-dimethylanilines containing halogen substituents and electron-releasing groups. Compatible with α-azidochalcones containing halogen, electron-withdrawing and electron-releasing groups. Does not work with electron-deficient anilines or heteroaromatic/aliphatic derived azidochalcones.

optimization_table.key_findings: Ru(bpy)3(PF6)2 with TBHP in CH3CN gave optimal 51% yield

figures.flow_path: reactants flow through PFA capillaries coiled inside glass column
figures.key_information: photochemical flow reactor for azide-alkene cycloaddition, 20 examples with 55-71% yields

figures.mechanism_type: visible-light photoredox oxidation of tertiary amines
key_observations_supporting_mechanism: One-sp³ C-H bond functionalization products not observed with azido carbonyl compounds
figures.caption: Scheme 1. Functionalization of  tert -Amines under Visible-Light Photoredox Conditions

figures.flow_path: two inlet streams merge into glass column reactor with helical PFA capillaries, then to product outlet
figures.reactor_geometry: helical coiled capillaries within glass column
figures.key_information: photochemical flow reactor with reactants 1a + 2a + TBHP in CH3CN and Ru(bpy)3(PF6)2 in CH3CN as inputs, producing product 3a
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Coupling of  N , N -dimethylaniline  1a  with  α -azidochalcone  2a  in a  flow microreactor. | Figure 2.  Scope of the visible-light driven coupling of  N , N -dimethylanilines  1  and  α -azidochalcones  2  to yield aziridine derivatives  3  in the  flow microreactor.

Figure 1.  Coupling of  N , N -dimethylaniline  1a  with  α -azidochalcone  2a  in a  flow microreactor. | Figure 2.  Scope of the visible-light driven coupling of  N , N -dimethylanilines  1  and  α -azidochalcones  2  to yield aziridine derivatives  3  in the  flow microreactor.

Scheme 2. Mechanistic Rationalization for the Formation of 1,3-Diazabicyclo[3.1.0]hexanes 3 via Visible-Light Driven Photocascade Catalysis | Scheme 3. Photosensitized Generation of 2 H -Azirine 4b and Its Coupling with  N , N ,4-Trimethylaniline 1d in a Photoredox/ [3+2] Cycloaddition Reaction Cascade

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction times (12 hours) and lower yields (51%) in batch conditions
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Improved mass transfer and light penetration in flow microreactor leading to higher yields and faster reactions
translation_logic.what_changed: Residence time reduced from 720 min to 56 min, concentration reduced from 0.5 M to 0.1 M
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same catalyst (Ru(bpy)3(PF6)2), oxidant (TBHP), solvent (CH3CN), and light source (17W white LED)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing reduces inventory of hazardous materials

figure_descriptions: Photochemical flow reactor with PFA capillaries coiled in glass column
Two-inlet system with external LED illumination
Scope table showing 20 examples with 55-71% yields

conflicts: Figure shows 3 inlets but pump described as 2-inlet system - resolved by using figure data for inlet count
notes: Well-documented photocascade flow chemistry study with clear batch-to-flow translation benefits. Some minor inconsistencies in inlet configuration between text and figures.
